title: Restore working real OCR (OCR.space) while keeping v18 app unchanged + ensure purple theme everywhere

requirements.text: Replace the current OCR “Offline Mode” placeholder flow with a real online OCR integration using a legal free-tier external OCR API (OCR.space) so that selecting an image/PDF and clicking “Extract Questions” performs actual text extraction and proceeds to the existing review/approve flow.

quotes: I want my full privious version back with real ocr
We can't make real ocr? If we can make it and don't change anything in my current app other than ocr and purple colour themea in my full app all pages and button

acceptanceCriteria: On /ocr/upload, the app can extract text from a user-selected supported file (image/PDF) via OCR.space over HTTPS (no manual “paste text” requirement to proceed).
The “Offline Mode: This app cannot perform real OCR extraction…” messaging is removed/replaced with accurate user-facing copy describing the real OCR process.

When OCR extraction succeeds, the extracted text is saved to the OCR session storage and the user is navigated to /ocr/review with parsed questions populated (using the existing text-to-questions parsing logic).
When OCR extraction fails (network/API error, unsupported file, quota exceeded), the user sees a clear error message and can retry or choose a different file without the rest of the app breaking.
No other routes/screens/features outside the OCR flow are removed or altered.

requirements.text: Update the OCR session utility currently named/mock-behaving as a mock extractor so it supports storing sessions created from real OCR results (extracted text + parsed questions) while keeping the existing session load/clear/convert APIs used by the OCR review screen.

quotes: I want my full privious version back with real ocr
don't change anything in my current app other than ocr

acceptanceCriteria: The OCR review page (/ocr/review) continues to work without route changes and can render questions originating from real OCR extraction.
Session persistence still uses the existing localStorage key and supports clear/load behavior so cancel/back flows work as before.
Approved questions can still be inserted into the personal question bank exactly as before.

requirements.text: Ensure a consistent purple theme across the entire app (all pages and buttons) by verifying that primary/secondary/accent tokens and button variants visually render as purple consistently in both light and dark mode, without changing layout or features.

quotes: purple colour themea in my full app all pages and button

acceptanceCriteria: Primary actions (default buttons/active states) visibly use the purple primary color across all pages, including navigation and key CTAs.
Theme is consistent in both light and dark mode (no pages/components falling back to non-purple default styling).
No UI screens are removed or restructured as part of theme adjustments (token-level/styling-only changes).

constraints: Do not remove or change any non-OCR screens/features from the current v18 app; only modify OCR behavior and global purple theme consistency.

Frontend immutable paths must not be edited: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.tsx, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
Backend remains single-actor (backend/main.mo); do not introduce additional backend services.
nonGoals: Adding new app modules or changing the home screen/paper editor flows beyond what is necessary for OCR and theme consistency.
Introducing third-party auth providers or real-time features.
Building an on-chain OCR solution (OCR must use an external API via HTTPS).
